title: Carduncellus arborescens
intro: Carduncellus arborescens, also known as artichoke thistle or tree cardoon, is a perennial plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including improving digestion, reducing inflammation, and supporting liver function, likely due to its antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties. The bioactive constituents of Carduncellus arborescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to support overall health and well-being.
